name: Temporal (uvx)
description: Manage Temporal workflows through natural language. Start, query, signal, cancel and inspect workflow executions, run batch operations, and manage schedules on a self-hosted Temporal cluster or Temporal Cloud.

inputs.label: Temporal Host
inputs.description: Temporal frontend address as host:port. Use localhost:7233 for a local cluster, or your Temporal Cloud gRPC endpoint such as <namespace>.<account>.tmprl.cloud:7233.

inputs.label: Temporal Namespace
inputs.description: Namespace to connect to. Defaults to 'default' for local clusters; on Temporal Cloud use your namespace id (e.g. my-namespace.a1b2c).

inputs.label: Temporal Cloud API Key
inputs.description: Temporal Cloud API key. Leave empty for a local/self-hosted cluster without auth. When set, TLS is enabled automatically.

obtain.instructions:
1. Sign in to Temporal Cloud
2. Go to Settings > API Keys
3. Generate a new API key
4. Copy the key value (shown only once)

inputs.label: Enable TLS
inputs.description: Set to true to force TLS. Leave empty to auto-detect (TLS turns on automatically when an API key or mTLS cert/key is provided).

inputs.label: mTLS Client Certificate Path
inputs.description: Path to the client certificate (PEM) for mTLS authentication. Use this instead of an API key when connecting with client certificates. Leave empty if not using mTLS.

inputs.label: mTLS Client Key Path
inputs.description: Path to the client private key (PEM) for mTLS authentication. Pair with the client certificate. Leave empty if not using mTLS.

auth.instructions: No auth is needed for a local Temporal cluster. For Temporal Cloud, provide a Temporal Cloud API key (TLS enables automatically), or use mTLS by providing the client certificate and key paths.
